notes: Gemini CLI has no built-in autonomous self-prompting mode (no /goal, no Stop-hook re-prompt, no max-iterations cap; YOLO mode only auto-approves tools and nudges the model, it does not re-prompt across turns). The loop is driven EXTERNALLY: the driver feeds successive prompts to one live `gemini -y` REPL session, each advancing one iteration of a single ongoing goal (count 1→2→3). The spec asserts what the irrlicht autonomous-loop primitive actually cares about for a turn-delimited adapter: each iteration cycles ready→working→ready cleanly on the SAME rollout session_id, the session never sticks in working and never flips to waiting (yolo auto-approves every tool → no permission gates), and the loop ends in ready. This mirrors the codex sibling (also externally driven) and relaxes claudecode's strict 'stays continuously working' span the same way (claudecode's /goal also flickers ready↔working per Stop-hook iteration). gemini-cli emits no explicit end-of-turn marker; turn_done is INFERRED from a final text-only 'gemini' message with non-empty content and no open tool calls (parser.go ~L209) — so each iter_done is emission-heuristic and PROVISIONAL until record settles it. session_birth anchors to start UNPINNED so a transient proc-<PID> presession can't steal the birth; each iter's _done anchors to its own _start (not session_birth) so the matcher picks the post-iteration ready, not an earlier handoff. observations.model is the generic Google id 'gemini' (no -m flag; live default is account-dependent) — cost+tokens nonzero still run.
source: .specs/agent-scenarios.md → Feature: Autonomous loop (self-prompting) — modeled as an externally-driven loop; cf. codex/claudecode siblings

text: proc-<PID> pre-session appears in ready within 1s of launch
Rollout session reconciles (different session_id, keyed on the transcript header sessionId), born ready
Launcher PID bound to the rollout session (cwd harvested from the bootstrap <session_context>)
Loop start: first externally-fed prompt → ready → working on the rollout session
First iteration completes → working → ready (state cycles cleanly between iterations, not stuck working)
Next loop iteration: driver re-prompts → ready → working on the same session — loop is active
Second iteration completes → back to ready
Final loop iteration: driver re-prompts → ready → working on the same session
Loop terminates: final iteration ends in ready; the session never entered waiting across the whole loop (gemini -y auto-approves every tool, no permission gates)
Driver tears down the REPL — process_exited on the rollout session
